name: steps
description: Visual progress indicator for multi-step flows with horizontal and vertical orientations

meta.name: Steps
meta.description: Visual progress indicator for multi-step flows with horizontal and vertical orientations

props.name: steps
props.description: Array of step objects with label and optional explicit state

props.name: activeStep
props.description: Index of the currently active step (0-based)

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

examples.title: Horizontal (default)
examples.description: Standard checkout or onboarding progress tracker
examples.code:
<Steps
  steps={[
    { label: 'Cart' },
    { label: 'Shipping' },
    { label: 'Payment' },
    { label: 'Confirm' },
  ]}
  activeStep={1}
/>

examples.title: Vertical
examples.code:
<Steps
  orientation="vertical"
  steps={[
    { label: 'Account info' },
    { label: 'Profile details' },
    { label: 'Preferences' },
  ]}
  activeStep={0}
/>

examples.title: With explicit error state
examples.description: Override derived state on a specific step
examples.code:
<Steps
  steps={[
    { label: 'Upload' },
    { label: 'Validate', state: 'error' },
    { label: 'Process' },
  ]}
  activeStep={1}
/>

whenToUse: Checkout flows where the user moves through a fixed sequence of screens
Onboarding wizards with a known number of steps
Multi-step forms where showing overall progress reduces abandonment
whenNotToUse: General section navigation — use Tabs instead
Simple back/next controls without step labels — use Pagination instead
More than 7 steps where the connector lines become unreadable on mobile

antiPatterns.bad: Using Steps as a replacement for Tabs for non-sequential navigation
antiPatterns.why: Steps imply a linear sequence and derive complete/pending state from position

related.name: Pagination
related.reason: Pagination is for paged data sets, Steps is for guided task sequences

related.name: Tabs
related.reason: Tabs are for non-sequential section switching, not ordered task flows

intent.a11yRationale: Rendered as an ordered list (<ol>) with aria-label; the active item carries aria-current="step" to communicate progress to screen readers

flexibility.note: Each step can override derived pending/active/complete state via step.state
Horizontal for top progress bars, vertical for sidebar wizards
